projectDescription: An open-source ESP32-powered wireless toolkit for scanning, jamming, spoofing, and attacking BLE, Wi-Fi, and 2.4GHz networks using NRF24L01 modules.
keyFeatures: 2.4GHz channel scanner with OLED spectrum display
Signal analyzer with real-time graphing via NRF24L01
WLAN jammer with configurable channel, PA level, and data rate
BLE jammer targeting BLE and classic Bluetooth channels
BLE spoofer simulating Apple (AirPods, Beats), Samsung Galaxy Watch, and Google devices
Sour Apple attack — spams fake Apple BLE proximity advertisements
Proto Kill mode for jamming WiFi, BLE, Bluetooth, Zigbee, RC, Video TX, USB wireless, and NRF24
Wi-Fi scanner listing nearby networks with SSID, BSSID, RSSI, and channel
BLE scanner listing nearby BLE devices with name and RSSI
Wi-Fi deauthentication attack sending raw 802.11 deauth frames
NeoPixel status LED with per-mode color feedback
OLED display with icon grid menu navigation
publicApiSurface: NRF_CE_PIN_A/B/C, NRF_CSN_PIN_A/B/C — three NRF24L01 radio module SPI pins
